passed.title: Week 1: Foundations
passed.description: Understanding objects, classes, and the basics of OOP

days.title: Day 1: Objects in Python
days.description: Everything is an object in Python

problems.title: Hello, Object!
problems.description: Explore Python's object nature by inspecting types and identities
hints: Use Python's built-in type() function to get the type of an object.
Remember that everything in Python is an object, including integers and strings.
Try calling type() on different values to see what you get.

problems.title: Type Detective
problems.description: Write a function that returns the type name of any object
hints: type(obj) gives you the type, but you need to get its name.
Type objects have a __name__ attribute.
You can access it like: type(obj).__name__

days.title: Day 2: Defining Classes
days.description: Create your own classes with attributes and methods

problems.title: Simple Person Class
problems.description: Create a Person class with name and age attributes
problems.test_code:
def test_person_creation():
    p = Person('Alice', 30)
    assert p.name == 'Alice'
    assert p.age == 30

def test_person_different_values():
    p = Person('Bob', 25)
    assert p.name == 'Bob'
    assert p.age == 25

def test_person_edge_case_zero_age():
    p = Person('Baby', 0)
    assert p.age == 0
hints: In the __init__ method, use self.name = name to set attributes.
Remember to use self to refer to instance attributes.
Don't forget to handle all parameters passed to __init__.

passed.title: Week 2: Encapsulation
passed.description: Private attributes, properties, and controlling access

days.title: Day 1: Private Attributes
days.description: Using underscores to indicate private members

problems.title: Bank Account
problems.description: Create a bank account with private balance and public methods
problems.test_code:
def test_initial_balance():
    acc = BankAccount()
    assert acc.balance == 0

def test_deposit():
    acc = BankAccount()
    acc.deposit(100)
    assert acc.balance == 100

def test_withdraw():
    acc = BankAccount()
    acc.deposit(100)
    acc.withdraw(30)
    assert acc.balance == 70

def test_multiple_operations():
    acc = BankAccount()
    acc.deposit(50)
    acc.deposit(25)
    acc.withdraw(10)
    assert acc.balance == 65
hints: Use self._balance to store the balance internally.
Update _balance in deposit and withdraw methods.
The balance property should just return self._balance.
